sections.title: Penalties for non-compliance with Central Bank directions
sections.summary: Institutions or individuals who fail to comply with Central Bank directions under the Act face monetary penalties. Institutions may be fined up to one million shillings, while natural persons face fines up to one hundred thousand shillings. Additional penalties of up to ten thousand shillings per day may be imposed for continued non-compliance.

requirements.text: Any institution failing to comply with Central Bank directions is liable to a penalty not exceeding one million shillings
Any natural person failing to comply with Central Bank directions is liable to a penalty not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings
Additional penalties not exceeding ten thousand shillings per day may be imposed for continued non-compliance

sections.source_quote: 2. (a) Any institution or other person who fails or refuses to comply with any directions given by the Central Bank under the Act shall be liable to a penalty not exceeding one million shillings in the case of an institution, or one hundred thousand shillings in the case of a natural person.

sections.heading: (1) In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires (cont.)
sections.title: Definitions of relationship terms for institutions
sections.summary: This section defines terms related to the relationships between institutions and their stakeholders, including 'associate', 'control', and their specific applications in the context of institutional management and ownership. It specifies how control is determined, including voting rights, influence over boards, and agreements that may grant dominant influence over management.
sections.dtia_summary: These definitions clarify the relationships between institutions and their stakeholders, which is critical for understanding data sharing and transfer mechanisms involving related entities. They help determine when data transfers between related entities require specific regulatory oversight.

requirements.text: For the purposes of this Act, 'associate' in relation to a company or other body corporate means its holding company or its subsidiary, a subsidiary of its holding company, a holding company of its subsidiary, its non-operating holding company as its subsidiary, a subsidiary of a non-operating holding company, and any person who controls the company or body corporate

In relation to an individual, 'associate' means any member of his family, any company or other body corporate controlled directly or indirectly by him, and any person who is deemed to be a member of a family

For purposes of subsection (2), the term 'control' includes the ability to influence board decisions, failure to comply with this Act, the Central Bank of Kenya Act, or any rules, regulations, orders or directions issued under those Acts, or any condition of a licence

sections.source_quote: 2 (part 2) For the purposes of this Act, 'associate' in relation to a company or other body corporate means its holding company or its subsidiary, a subsidiary of its holding company, a holding company of its subsidiary, its non-operating holding company as its subsidiary, a subsidiary of a non-operating holding company, and any person who controls the company or body corporate

sections.title: Resignation of liquidator by Board upon notice to Official Receiver
sections.summary: The Board can resign as liquidator of an institution by providing written notice to the Official Receiver. sections.title: Liquidator's duty to pay depositor excess after insolvency payment
sections.summary: The liquidator of an insolvent banking institution must pay the Board the amount specified in regulation (8) instead of depositors. If this payment equals the insolvency payment made to depositors by the Board, the liquidator must then pay any excess amount to depositors. This applies to liquidators handling insolvent banking institutions.

sections.title: Minimum liquid assets requirement for banking institutions
sections.summary: Banking institutions in Kenya must maintain a minimum holding of liquid assets as determined by the Central Bank. Non-compliance incurs a penalty interest charge of up to 1% per day on the deficiency.

sections.title: Auditor appointment and reporting duties for financial institutions
sections.summary: Financial institutions must appoint an auditor qualified under section 161 and approved by the Central Bank. The auditor must audit annual financial statements and submit reports to the Central Bank. If an institution fails to appoint an auditor, the Central Bank may appoint one. The Central Bank may require additional duties from auditors including special investigations and reports. Auditors who fail to comply may be removed by the Central Bank.

sections.title: Central Bank's powers to issue directions and enforce compliance
sections.summary: The Central Bank may issue directions to institutions to improve compliance, cease practices, or appoint advisors if it believes an institution is acting contrary to the Act or in the public's best interest. The Central Bank may also restrict dividends, prohibit profit conversions, remove officers, reconstitute boards, and require capital restoration plans if an institution is undercapitalized or non-compliant. Failure to comply with directions results in penalties.
sections.dtia_summary: Provides extensive enforcement powers; critical for DTIA to assess regulatory oversight and corrective actions for data handling failures.

requirements.text: Central Bank may issue directions to improve management or compliance with the Act
Central Bank may issue directions to cease practices likely to contravene the Act
Central Bank may appoint advisors to assist institutions in implementing directions
Central Bank may restrict dividends if an institution is non-compliant
Central Bank may prohibit profit conversions into capital
Central Bank may remove officers involved in contraventions
Central Bank may require reconstitution of boards of directors
Central Bank may withhold branch approvals
Central Bank may order capital restoration plans to meet capital adequacy standards
Central Bank may impose restrictions on asset growth
Central Bank may restrict interest rates on deposits

sections.source_quote: The Central Bank may issue directions to institutions generally for the better carrying out of its functions under this Act and in particular, with respect to the standards to be adhered to by an institution in the conduct of its business in Kenya or in any country where a branch or subsidiary of the institution is located.

sections.heading: (1)    This section applies, and the powers conferred by
sections.title: Central Bank's intervention powers in financial distress
sections.summary: The Central Bank may intervene in institutions that fail financial obligations, are wound up, or are undercapitalized. It may appoint the Kenya Deposit Insurance Corporation to manage the institution, remove officers, restrict foreign exchange business, prohibit off-balance sheet transactions, and terminate agency arrangements. The Central Bank may also approve voluntary liquidations if institutions meet all liabilities.
sections.dtia_summary: Provides intervention powers for financial distress; critical for DTIA to assess data handling during institutional failures.

requirements.text: Central Bank may appoint Kenya Deposit Insurance Corporation to manage an institution
Central Bank may remove officers who caused contraventions or deteriorated financial stability
Central Bank may restrict new foreign exchange business
Central Bank may prohibit new off-balance sheet transactions
Central Bank may prohibit new agents or direct termination of agency arrangements
Institutions may voluntarily liquidate with Central Bank approval if they meet all liabilities
Central Bank may approve voluntary liquidations if satisfied with the institution's solvency

sections.source_quote: The Central Bank may appoint Kenya Deposit Insurance Corporation to assume the management, control and conduct of the affairs and business of an institution and to exercise all the powers of the institution to the exclusion of its board of directors including the use of its corporate seal.

sections.heading: (1) The Central Bank may, in writing and subject to such
sections.title: Central Bank authorization for foreign representative offices
sections.summary: The Central Bank may authorize foreign financial institutions to establish representative offices in Kenya under specific conditions. The authorization requires written application, information submission, and compliance with Central Bank directives regarding business activities. The Central Bank can also close the office if it breaches conditions.
sections.dtia_summary: This section governs foreign entities establishing representative offices in Kenya, which may involve cross-border data transfers. The Central Bank's oversight ensures compliance with local financial regulations for foreign entities operating in Kenya.

requirements.text: The Central Bank may authorize a bank or financial institution incorporated outside Kenya to open a representative office in Kenya
The Central Bank may require a representative office to furnish information as directed
The representative office must furnish information within the specified period or agreed reasonable period
The Central Bank may issue directions to the representative office to take corrective action if it engages in banking or financial business contrary to conditions
The Central Bank may order the closure of a representative office if it fails to comply with directions

sections.source_quote: 43. (1) The Central Bank may, in writing and subject to such conditions as the Central Bank may consider necessary, authorize a bank or a financial institution incorporated outside Kenya which does not propose to transact banking or financial business in Kenya but which proposes and applies in writing to the Central Bank to establish a representative office in Kenya to open an office in a place in Kenya approved by the Central Bank.

sections.heading: No institution shall increase its rate of banking or other
sections.title: Limitation on interest recovery for non-performing loans
sections.summary: Institutions are limited in the interest they can recover from debtors on non-performing loans. The maximum amount includes principal, interest not exceeding principal, and recovery expenses. The limit applies to loans that became non-performing before the section came into effect, with specific calculations for such cases.
sections.dtia_summary: This section sets limits on interest recovery for non-performing loans, which may impact financial data handling and cross-border loan operations involving Kenyan institutions.

requirements.text: An institution shall be limited in what it may recover from a debtor with respect to a non-performing loan to the maximum amount under subsection (2)
The maximum amount includes the principal owing when the loan becomes non-performing, interest not exceeding the principal, and expenses incurred in recovery
If a loan becomes non-performing again after resuming payments, the limitation applies to the time the loan last became non-performing
This section does not apply to interest under court orders made after the order is issued
The definition of 'debtor' includes persons indebted through guarantees
The definition of 'loan' includes advances, credit facilities, financial guarantees, and other liabilities
A loan becomes non-performing in a manner stipulated by Central Bank guidelines

sections.source_quote: 44. No institution shall increase its rate of banking or other charges except with the prior approval of the Minister. 44A. (1) An institution shall be limited in what it may recover from a debtor with respect to a non-performing loan to the maximum amount under subsection (2)

sections.heading: (1)   The Minister shall consult with the Central Bank in the
sections.title: Minister consults Central Bank on functions
sections.summary: The Minister must consult with the Central Bank before exercising any functions under the Banking Act. Applications for Ministerial approval must be submitted through the Central Bank. This section establishes the Central Bank as the primary channel for Ministerial approvals and consultations.

requirements.text: The Minister shall consult with the Central Bank in the exercise of his functions under this Act
Where the approval of the Minister is required under any provision of this Act, the application for such approval shall be submitted through the Central Bank

sections.source_quote: 45. (1) The Minister shall consult with the Central Bank in the exercise of his functions under this Act. (2) Where the approval of the Minister is required under any provision of this Act, the application for such approval shall be submitted through the Central Bank.

sections.heading: (1) Where the Minister considers that it is in the public interest
sections.title: Minister declares bank holidays for non-public holidays
sections.summary: The Minister may declare non-public holidays as bank holidays through the Gazette if it serves the public interest. The Central Bank can also authorize specific banks or branches to close on non-public holidays under certain conditions. This section provides mechanisms for temporary bank closures during non-public holidays.

requirements.text: The Minister may by notice in the Gazette declare that day to be a bank holiday for all banks, or for that particular bank, or for that particular branch
The Central Bank may authorize an institution or branch to remain closed on such day or part thereof, subject to terms and conditions

sections.source_quote: 46. (1) Where the Minister considers that it is in the public interest that banks, or a particular bank, or a particular branch of a bank, should remain closed on a day which is not a public holiday, he may by notice in the Gazette, declare that day to be a bank holiday for all banks, or for that particular bank, or for that particular branch, as the case may be, and every licensed bank, or that particular bank, or that particular branch, as the case may be, shall remain closed on that day.

sections.heading: (1) The High Court, on application made ex-parte by the
sections.title: High Court orders for depositor protection
sections.summary: The High Court can order a bank to cease business or stay legal proceedings against it for up to six months if it serves depositors' interests. Such orders are made ex-parte by the Minister or a Central Bank-appointed manager/liquidator. The bank's license is suspended during such orders.

requirements.text: The High Court may prohibit the institution from carrying on business
The High Court may stay the commencement or continuance of any actions or proceedings against the institution for a specified period up to six months

sections.source_quote: 47. (1) The High Court, on application made ex-parte by the Minister or, where a manager or liquidator has been appointed by the Central Bank, may, if it considers it to be in the interests of the depositors of an institution, make an order: (a) prohibiting the institution from carrying on business; or (b) staying the commencement or continuance of any actions or proceedings against the institution in regard to any business for a specified period of time on such terms and conditions as it considers reasonable, and may from time to time extend the specified period up to a total of six months from the beginning of the stay.

sections.heading: (1) A person who is an officer of an institution shall cease to
sections.title: Officers disqualified for bankruptcy or fraud
sections.summary: Officers of financial institutions lose eligibility to hold office if they become bankrupt, suspend payment, compound with creditors, are convicted of dishonesty or fraud, or are disqualified under section 32A. Continuing to act after disqualification is an offence.

requirements.text: A person who is an officer of an institution shall cease to hold office and shall not thereafter be eligible to hold office in any institution if he becomes bankrupt or suspends payment or compounds with his creditors

A person who is an officer of an institution shall cease to hold office and shall not thereafter be eligible to hold office in any institution if he is convicted of an offence involving dishonesty or fraud

A person who is an officer of an institution shall cease to hold office and shall not thereafter be eligible to hold office in any institution if he is disqualified from holding office under section 32A

A person who is an officer of an institution shall cease to hold office and shall not thereafter be eligible to hold office in any institution if he is removed from office under the provisions of section 34

sections.source_quote: 48. (1) A person who is an officer of an institution shall cease to hold office and shall not thereafter be eligible to hold office in any institution if he: (a) becomes bankrupt or suspends payment or compounds with his creditors; or (b) is convicted of an offence involving dishonesty or fraud; or (bb) is disqualified from holding office under section 32A; or (c) is removed from office under the provisions of section 34.

sections.heading: Where any institution or other person contravenes any of the
sections.title: Penalties for contravening Banking Act
sections.summary: Institutions and officers face fines and imprisonment for contravening the Banking Act. Bodies corporate can be fined up to 100,000 shillings, while officers face fines up to 50,000 shillings or imprisonment for two years. Officers may claim ignorance as a defense if they took reasonable steps to prevent contraventions.

requirements.text: If it is a body corporate, it shall be guilty of an offence and liable to a fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings
Every officer of that institution or person shall be guilty of an offence and liable to a fine not exceeding fifty thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding two years or to both

Unless he proves that, through no act or omission on his part, he was not aware that the contravention was taking place or was intended or about to take place, or that he took all reasonable steps to prevent it taking place

sections.source_quote: 49. Where any institution or other person contravenes any of the provisions of this Act: (a) if it is a body corporate, it shall be guilty of an offence and liable to a fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings; and (b) every officer of that institution or person shall be guilty of an offence and liable to a fine not exceeding fifty thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding two years or to both unless he proves that, through no act or omission on his part, he was not aware that the contravention was taking place or was intended or about to take place, or that he took all reasonable steps to prevent it taking place.

sections.heading: (1) Any officer of an institution who:-
sections.title: Officers liable for non-compliance
sections.summary: Officers of institutions face imprisonment up to one year or fines up to 20,000 shillings for failing to ensure compliance with the Act, inaccurate statements, or supplying required information. An officer may claim ignorance if they reasonably believed another competent person was responsible.

requirements.text: Any officer of an institution who fails to take all reasonable steps to secure the compliance of the institution with this Act shall be guilty of an offence and liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding one year or to a fine not exceeding twenty thousand shillings or to both

Any officer of an institution who fails to take all reasonable steps to secure the accuracy and correctness of any statement submitted under this Act or any other written law applicable to banks or financial institutions shall be guilty of an offence and liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding one year or to a fine not exceeding twenty thousand shillings or to both

Any officer of an institution who fails to supply any information required under this Act shall be guilty of an offence and liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding one year or to a fine not exceeding twenty thousand shillings or to both

It shall be a defense to a charge under subsection (1) for an officer to show that he reasonably thought that another competent person had been charged with the responsibility or duty in respect of which the default arose

sections.source_quote: 50. (1) Any officer of an institution who: (a) fails to take all reasonable steps to secure the compliance of the institution with this Act; or (b) fails to take all reasonable steps to secure the accuracy and correctness of any statement submitted under this Act or any other written law applicable to banks or financial institutions; or (c) fails to supply any information required under this Act, shall be guilty of an offence and liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding one year or to a fine not exceeding twenty thousand shillings or to both. (2) It shall be a defence to a charge under subsection (1) for an officer to show that he reasonably thought that another competent person had been charged with the responsibility or duty in respect of which the default arose.

sections.heading: (1) Any institution or other person who issues any
sections.title: Prohibition on misleading deposit advertisements
sections.summary: Any institution or person advertising deposits must not falsely claim authorization to accept deposits or be licensed under the Act. Violators face criminal penalties. The Central Bank may direct withdrawal or amendment of misleading deposit advertisements. This section applies to all entities promoting deposit products in Kenya.
sections.dtia_summary: Prohibits misleading deposit advertising that could mislead customers about authorization status, relevant for assessing whether deposit promotions comply with local licensing requirements before cross-border data transfers involving financial services.

requirements.text: Any institution or other person who issues any advertisement, brochure, circular or other document inviting any person to make a deposit must not falsely represent that he is authorized to accept deposits or is otherwise licensed under the provisions of this Act

Any institution or other person who issues any advertisement, brochure, circular or other document inviting any person to make a deposit must not be issued contrary to any direction given by the Central Bank under the provisions of subsection (2)

sections.source_quote: 51. (1) Any institution or other person who issues any advertisement, brochure, circular or other document inviting any person to make a deposit which:- (a) falsely represents that he is authorized to accept deposits or is otherwise licensed under the provisions of this Act; or (b) is issued contrary to any direction given by the Central Bank under the provisions of subsection (2), shall be guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: (1)  For the avoidance of doubt, no contravention of  the
sections.title: Contractual obligations prevail over conflicting laws
sections.summary: Contractual obligations between institutions and other persons remain valid even if they conflict with this Act or the Central Bank of Kenya Act. This section applies retroactively to repealed laws and prevents institutions from recovering interest exceeding legal limits. It also specifies that this Act prevails over other written laws applicable to licensed institutions, excluding certain specific laws.
sections.dtia_summary: Clarifies that contractual obligations are protected from being invalidated by this Act, relevant for assessing whether cross-border data transfers are covered by existing contracts that might conflict with local data regulations.

requirements.text: No contravention of the provisions of this Act or the Central Bank of Kenya Act shall affect or invalidate any contractual obligation between an institution and any other person
The provisions of subsection (1) shall apply with retrospective effect to the Banking Act (now repealed) and the Central Bank of Kenya Act

This section shall not permit any institution to recover in any court of law interest and other charges which exceed the maximum permitted under the provisions of this Act or the Central Bank of Kenya Act
Where there is a conflict between the provisions of this Act and the provisions of any other written law applicable to an institution licensed under this Act, the provisions of this Act shall prevail

For the purposes of subsection (1), the expression 'written law' does not include the Central Bank of Kenya Act, Income Tax Act, the East African Community Customs Management Act, the Value Added Tax Act or any of the other laws set out in the First Schedule to the Kenya Revenue Authority Act

sections.source_quote: 52. (1) For the avoidance of doubt, no contravention of the provisions of this Act or the Central Bank of Kenya Act shall affect or invalidate in any way any contractual obligation between an institution and any other person.

sections.heading: (1) The Minister may, by notice in the Gazette, exempt an
sections.title: Exemptions for specific institutions
sections.summary: The Minister may exempt institutions from Sections 12, 13, or 14 of the Act by Gazette notice, subject to conditions the Minister deems necessary. Exemptions remain in force for the period specified in the notice. This section applies to institutions seeking relief from specific regulatory requirements.
sections.dtia_summary: Allows for exemptions from certain regulatory requirements, relevant for assessing whether specific institutions are exempt from data transfer obligations when handling financial data.

requirements.text: The Minister may, by notice in the Gazette, exempt an institution from the provisions of Section 12, 13 or 14 subject to such conditions as the Minister considers necessary
An exemption granted under subsection (1) shall remain in force for such period specified in the notice as the Minister shall deem fit

sections.source_quote: 53. (1) The Minister may, by notice in the Gazette, exempt an institution from the provisions of Section 12, 13 or 14 subject to such conditions as the Minister considers necessary.

sections.heading: (1) This Act does not apply to:-
sections.title: Excluded institutions from the Act
sections.summary: This Act does not apply to the Kenya Post Office Savings Bank, Agricultural Finance Corporation, co-operative societies, and microfinance banks licensed under the Microfinance Act, 2006. However, if these entities act as agents for institutions providing banking services, the Act applies to them for the services contracted.
sections.dtia_summary: Identifies specific institutions exempt from the Act, relevant for determining whether data transfers involving these entities require additional compliance measures.

requirements.text: This Act does not apply to the Kenya Post Office Savings Bank established under the Kenya Post Office Savings Bank Act
This Act does not apply to the Agricultural Finance Corporation established under the Agricultural Finance Corporation Act
This Act does not apply to a society registered as a co-operative society under the Co-operative Societies Act
This Act does not apply to a microfinance bank licensed under the Microfinance Act, 2006

Where any of the bodies referred to in subsection (1) is contracted by an institution as an agent to provide banking services on behalf of the institution, this Act shall apply to such body to the extent of the services contracted

sections.source_quote: 54. (1) This Act does not apply to:- (a) the Kenya Post Office Savings Bank established under the Kenya Post Office Savings Bank Act; (b) the Agricultural Finance Corporation established under the Agricultural Finance Corporation Act; (d) a society registered as a co-operative society under the Co-operative Societies Act. (e) a microfinance bank licensed under the Microfinance Act, 2006.

sections.heading: (1) The Central Bank may make regulations generally for
sections.title: Regulations and penalties for non-compliance
sections.summary: The Central Bank may make regulations to implement this Act, including prescribing penalties for institutions or credit reference bureaus that fail to comply with Central Bank directions. Penalties for institutions range up to five million Kenyan shillings, while natural persons face penalties up to two hundred thousand shillings, with additional penalties for each day of non-compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: Specifies penalties for non-compliance with Central Bank directions, relevant for assessing the financial impact of regulatory breaches in data transfer contexts.

requirements.text: The Central Bank may make regulations generally for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Act

The Central Bank may, in regulations, prescribe penalties to be paid by institutions or credit reference bureaus (or any other person) that fail or refuse to comply with any directions of the Central Bank under this Act or Prudential Guidelines
Penalties for institutions or credit reference bureaus shall not exceed five million Kenya shillings
Penalties for natural persons shall not exceed two hundred thousand shillings
Additional penalties not exceeding twenty thousand shillings in each case for each day or part thereof during which such failure or refusal continues

sections.source_quote: 55. (1) The Central Bank may make regulations generally for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Act. (2) Without prejudice to the generality of subsection (1), the Central Bank may, in regulations, prescribe penalties to be paid by institutions or credit reference bureaus (or any other person) that fail or refuse to comply with any directions of the Central Bank under this Act or Prudential Guidelines, which shall not exceed five million Kenya shillings in the case of an institution or credit reference bureau or two hundred thousand shillings in the case of a natural person, and may prescribe additional penalties not exceeding twenty thousand shillings in each case for each day or part thereof during which such failure or refusal continues.

sections.heading: (1) The Banking Act (1989) is repealed.
sections.title: Repeal of the 1989 Banking Act
sections.summary: The Banking Act (1989) is repealed, but existing licenses granted under it continue to be effective as if granted under Section 5 of this Act. This section ensures continuity for institutions licensed before the new Act came into effect.
sections.dtia_summary: Repeals the 1989 Banking Act, relevant for determining the applicability of the current Act to historical data transfers and institutional licenses.

requirements.text: The Banking Act (1989) is repealed

sections.title: Penalties for non-compliance with Central Bank directions
sections.summary: Institutions or individuals who fail to comply with Central Bank directions under the Act face monetary penalties. sections.heading: These Regulations may be cited as the Banking (Penalties) Regulation 1999
sections.title: Citation and scope of Banking (Penalties) Regulations 1999
sections.summary: These regulations are titled the Banking (Penalties) Regulation 1999 and apply to all banks and other institutions licensed under the Banking Act to conduct business in Kenya. The regulations establish the legal framework for penalties related to banking activities.

requirements.text: These Regulations may be cited as the Banking (Penalties) Regulation 1999
The Regulations apply to all banks and other institutions licensed under the Act to conduct business in Kenya

sections.source_quote: 1. These Regulations may be cited as the Banking (Penalties) Regulation 1999 and shall apply to all banks and other institutions licensed under the Act to conduct business in Kenya

sections.heading: (a) Any institution or other person who fails or refuses to comply with any
sections.title: Penalties for non-compliance with Central Bank directions
sections.summary: Institutions or individuals who fail to comply with Central Bank directions under the Act face monetary penalties. Institutions may be fined up to one million shillings, while natural persons face fines up to one hundred thousand shillings. Additional penalties of up to ten thousand shillings per day may be imposed for continued non-compliance.

requirements.text: Any institution failing to comply with Central Bank directions is liable to a penalty not exceeding one million shillings
Any natural person failing to comply with Central Bank directions is liable to a penalty not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings
Additional penalties not exceeding ten thousand shillings per day may be imposed for continued non-compliance

sections.source_quote: 2. (a) Any institution or other person who fails or refuses to comply with any directions given by the Central Bank under the Act shall be liable to a penalty not exceeding one million shillings in the case of an institution, or one hundred thousand shillings in the case of a natural person.

sections.heading: (1) The following shall constitute specific violations by an institution of the
sections.title: Specific violations subject to monetary penalties
sections.summary: This section lists specific violations by institutions that can be subject to monetary penalties. These include lending beyond 25% of core capital, unsecured advances to employees or associates, loans to officers without proper approval, and failure to submit annual audited accounts to the Central Bank within specified timelines.

requirements.text: Loans exceeding 25% of the institution's core capital to any person
Unsecured advances to employees or their associates
Unsecured loans to officers or their associates
Loans to persons with whom officers have an interest as agents, principals, directors, managers, or shareholders
Loans to persons with whom officers are guarantors
Loans to directors or other management participants without board approval
Loans to directors or management participants not made on terms similar to ordinary customers
Loans to directors or management participants not reported to the Central Bank within seven days
Aggregate credit facilities to shareholders, directors, officers, or employees exceeding 20% of core capital
Aggregate loans to shareholders, directors, officers, and employees exceeding 100% of core capital
Failure to exhibit annual audited accounts in conspicuous places in every office and branch
Failure to publish annual audited accounts in a national newspaper within prescribed months
Failure to submit annual audited accounts and auditor's report to the Central Bank within three months of financial year end
Failure to provide information to the Central Bank as required

sections.source_quote: 3. (1) The following shall constitute specific violations by an institution of the directions given by the Central Bank which shall be subject to assessment of monetary penalties under these Regulations:-

sections.heading: (a) The Central Bank, after reviewing all available information and
sections.title: Central Bank notification of penalty assessment
sections.summary: The Central Bank must notify an institution in writing after reviewing information and determining violations. The notification specifies the penalty assessment and provides a reasonable time frame for rectifying the violation. This ensures transparency in penalty enforcement procedures.

requirements.text: The Central Bank notifies the institution in writing of its findings and penalty decision
The notification specifies a reasonable time frame for rectifying the violation

sections.source_quote: 4. (a) The Central Bank, after reviewing all available information and determining the existence of the contravention or violations of one or more of the provisions referred to herein, shall notify the institution in writing advising it of its findings and its decision to assess the penalties. (b) A notification under (a) above shall advise the institution of a reasonable time frame within which the violation shall be rectified.

sections.heading: Following the notification and expiration of the time frame designated in
sections.title: Penalty assessment and payment procedures
sections.summary: Following notification and the expiration of the rectification period, the Central Bank instructs the institution on the penalty amount and payment method. If the institution fails to rectify the violation, daily penalties continue to accrue until rectification is complete.

requirements.text: The Central Bank instructs the institution on the penalty amount and payment method after rectification period
Daily penalties continue to accrue if the institution fails to rectify the violation
Daily penalties cease once the Central Bank is satisfied the violation has been rectified

sections.source_quote: 5. Following the notification and expiration of the time frame designated in (4) above, or sooner if advised by the institution of the correction of the violation, Director of Bank Supervision of the Central Bank shall instruct the institution, in writing, as to the amount of monetary penalties assessed and the manner in which such monies shall be paid to the Central Bank.
